fix: worktree-aware session discovery

CLI now uses git rev-parse --show-toplevel to find .groundcrew/
root, then walks up directories as fallback. Works from
subdirectories and git worktrees.

Hooks now use git rev-parse for consistent root detection.
Banner updated: Shift+Enter instead of Ctrl+J.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix setup.sh: download OSM file as map.osm.pbf (name GraphHopper expects), fix authorized_keys command

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add v14→v15 migration for users who ran browser-companion build

The browser-companion feature branch used migrations v12→v13 and
v13→v14 for its own tables, bumping the DB to user_version=14.
Users who ran that build have a DB at v14, so the existing
v12→v13 OneDrive migration never fires.

This adds a parallel v14→v15 migration block with identical OneDrive
table definitions (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) for those users.
Fresh installs are now set to user_version=15.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: runtime color theme system (get_sys_color / set_sys_colors) (#89)

* feat: add runtime color theme system (get_sys_color / set_sys_colors)

- Add sys_color_idx_t enum (22 slots) and get_sys_color() macro to messages.h
- Keep legacy COLOR_* macros as default-value constants for backward compat
- Implement g_sys_colors[] default table and set_sys_colors() in new user/theme.c
- New slots: kColorActiveTitlebar, kColorActiveTitlebarText,
  kColorInactiveTitlebar, kColorInactiveTitlebarText (focused vs unfocused),
  kColorWorkspaceBg (darker canvas/document area), kColorFolderText
- draw_window_controls() now uses active/inactive titlebar color based on focus
- Title text drawn in active/inactive titlebar text color based on focus
- commctl/image.c workspace uses kColorWorkspaceBg (distinct from statusbar)
- commctl/filelist.c folder color moved into theme table (kColorFolderText)
- All COLOR_* usages in user/ and commctl/ replaced with get_sys_color()

repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its central design philosophy is to faithfully reproduce the Windows API (Win32) message-based programming model in a portable, hardware-accelerated context.

commit.message:
fix(jetson): find trtexec in known paths when not in PATH

trtexec on Jetson is typically at /usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec
which is not in systemd service PATH. Now searches known locations
before falling back to error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add DB migration v12→v13 for OneDrive tables

Existing databases at user_version=12 missed the onedrive_roots,
onedrive_customer_folders, and onedrive_files tables since they were
only added to the fresh-install schema (getSchema). This migration
applies those tables to all existing databases on next startup.
Also bumps the fresh-install user_version to 13.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat/task 07 ml model registry (#19)

* feat: add ML model registry with ONNX and scikit-learn support (task 07)

- Add ModelRegistry for registering and retrieving ML models by name
- Add ONNXModel with lazy loading via onnxruntime and empty-schema passthrough
- Add SklearnModel with joblib/pickle loading, feature_order reordering, security warning
- Add ModelProvider protocol and ModelValidationResult frozen dataclass
- Add four new exceptions: ModelNotFoundError, ModelLoadError, ModelInputError, ModelInferenceError
- Wire ctx.predict() into BacktestEngine via model_registry param on _BacktestContext
- Add validation step 7 (Model Compatibility) via ModelCheck with AST scanning
- Extend nexa run and nexa validate CLI with --model name:path flag
- Add ONNX fixture generator script and pre-built simple_predictor.onnx fixture
- Add ml_da_algo.py example showing end-to-end ML workflow
- Add 50 comprehensive tests across 16 test classes (633 total, 99% coverage)
- Export new types from top-level nexa_backtest package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard sharpe ratio against single-observation std dev (ddof=1 with n=1)

Require at least 2 return observations before calling np.std(ddof=1).
With only 1 return value, numpy emits a RuntimeWarning (degrees of
freedom <= 0) and produces NaN. Returns None early instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: clean up model loading and validation logic

- SklearnModel._load(): wrap the entire joblib/pickle load block in a
  single try/except so errors from both loaders are caught and wrapped
  in ModelLoadError (previously pickle.load() errors could escape unwrapped)
- SklearnModel.validate(): remove redundant (ModelLoadError, Exception)
  — Exception already covers ModelLoadError
- model_check.py: replace fragile string-based failure detection
  ('FAILED' in m) with an explicit boolean flag
- _find_predict_calls(): use dict for O(1) deduplication instead of
  O(n) list membership checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update poetry.lock

* Improve code coverage

* Trigger build

* fix ruff checks

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: push images with :latest tag instead of Aspire timestamp tag

aspire do push defaults to aspire-deploy-YYYYMMDDHHMMSS tags. WithRemoteImageTag('latest')
ensures pushed images match the :latest references in the server's .env file.

commit.message:

commit.message:
CI: use aspire do push + aspire publish instead of manual docker build steps

aspire do push handles all three images (graphhopper Dockerfile, .NET api/web)
via the AppHost's AddContainerRegistry config. Much cleaner than separate
docker/build-push-action + dotnet publish /t:PublishContainer steps.

aspire publish regenerates docker-compose.yaml fresh from the AppHost model
each deploy — no need to update the committed file manually.

commit.message:
feat(ci): implement bench-source CI guard (Issue #4, Maßnahme #8) (#4600)

- Add tools/check_bench_targets.py: standalone guard that detects bench_*.cpp
  files without CMake target coverage. A source is covered by either an explicit
  add_executable() entry or the THEMIS_AUTO_REGISTER_ELIGIBLE_BENCHMARKS block.
  Supports --strict mode (requires explicit targets), --format json, --no-color.

- Add tools/perf_expectations_audit.py: audit orchestrator with STATUS_FAIL checks:
  * Check 8a: orphaned_sources == [] (PASS: 81 auto-registered, 0 orphaned)
  * Check 8b: tools/check_bench_targets.py exists (PASS)

- Add .github/workflows/05-quality_build_bench-source-guard-ci.yml: CI workflow
  that runs both tools on PR/push/schedule. Fails hard on new orphaned sources.

commit.message:
fix: add Ctrl+J as universal newline key for multiline input

Most macOS terminals (Terminal.app, iTerm2) send identical \r for both
Enter and Shift+Enter — there's no way to distinguish them. Only Kitty
and WezTerm support the Kitty keyboard protocol (\x1b[13;2u).

Fix: Add Ctrl+J (\n / LF) as cross-terminal newline. In raw mode, Enter
sends \r (CR) while Ctrl+J sends \n (LF), making them distinguishable
on ALL terminal emulators.

Newline options:
- Ctrl+J: Works everywhere (recommended)
- Shift+Enter: Works on Kitty/WezTerm (Kitty protocol)
- Alt+Enter: Works on iTerm2 and some terminals
- Backslash + Enter: Always works (manual continuation)

Also: added multiline hint to banner UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: fetch actual card images via DuckDuckGo + Bing (no API keys)

- scripts/fetch_card_images.py — searches DDG and Bing for real card photos
  - Scores results by domain reliability (Heritage Auctions, PSA, Beckett preferred)
  - Exponential backoff on DDG rate limits, automatic Bing fallback
  - Graceful shutdown (SIGINT/SIGTERM), incremental DB saves
  - 100% hit rate on first 95 cards tested
- Fix: /top endpoint now includes image_url in response (was missing)

Usage:
  python3 scripts/fetch_card_images.py              # Fetch all missing
  python3 scripts/fetch_card_images.py --progress   # Show stats
  python3 scripts/fetch_card_images.py --limit 100  # First 100 only

feat: ML model registry with ONNX and scikit-learn support (task 07) (#18)

* feat: add ML model registry with ONNX and scikit-learn support (task 07)

- Add ModelRegistry for registering and retrieving ML models by name
- Add ONNXModel with lazy loading via onnxruntime and empty-schema passthrough
- Add SklearnModel with joblib/pickle loading, feature_order reordering, security warning
- Add ModelProvider protocol and ModelValidationResult frozen dataclass
- Add four new exceptions: ModelNotFoundError, ModelLoadError, ModelInputError, ModelInferenceError
- Wire ctx.predict() into BacktestEngine via model_registry param on _BacktestContext
- Add validation step 7 (Model Compatibility) via ModelCheck with AST scanning
- Extend nexa run and nexa validate CLI with --model name:path flag
- Add ONNX fixture generator script and pre-built simple_predictor.onnx fixture
- Add ml_da_algo.py example showing end-to-end ML workflow
- Add 50 comprehensive tests across 16 test classes (633 total, 99% coverage)
- Export new types from top-level nexa_backtest package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard sharpe ratio against single-observation std dev (ddof=1 with n=1)

Require at least 2 return observations before calling np.std(ddof=1).
With only 1 return value, numpy emits a RuntimeWarning (degrees of
freedom <= 0) and produces NaN. Returns None early instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: clean up model loading and validation logic

- SklearnModel._load(): wrap the entire joblib/pickle load block in a
  single try/except so errors from both loaders are caught and wrapped
  in ModelLoadError (previously pickle.load() errors could escape unwrapped)
- SklearnModel.validate(): remove redundant (ModelLoadError, Exception)
  — Exception already covers ModelLoadError
- model_check.py: replace fragile string-based failure detection
  ('FAILED' in m) with an explicit boolean flag
- _find_predict_calls(): use dict for O(1) deduplication instead of
  O(n) list membership checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update poetry.lock

---------

commit.message:
feat: animations controlled exclusively by Animation Studio

- Sidebar: remove per-object 'Animate' toggle, 'Creation Animation' accordion
  and 'Animate All' global toggle — Studio is now the sole animation control
- InteractiveSvgCanvas: remove animAngle RAF loop and globalAnimating-based
  rotation; objects render rotation from obj.rotation set by Studio keyframes
- InteractiveCanvas: remove animAngleRef auto-spin; remove angle param from
  drawObject; canvas RAF loop already runs every frame so Studio updates
  applied via updateObject() are picked up instantly
- All 33 unit tests still pass, build clean

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add immutable username field with tenant-prefixed Keycloak username (#21)

- Add Username property to ApplicationUser model + EF migration
- Add Username to CreateUserRequest DTO (optional, defaults to email local part)
- Add Username to UserResponse and UserWithTenantsResponse DTOs
- Username is immutable: editable only during creation, read-only on edit
- Keycloak username format: {tenantId}.{username} for cross-tenant uniqueness

commit.message:
Add dependency CVE scanner via OSV.dev

New 'deps' command that scans NuGet and npm package manifests for
known vulnerabilities using the OSV.dev API.

Features:
- Manifest discovery: *.csproj, *.fsproj, *.vbproj, packages.lock.json,
  package-lock.json (v1/v2/v3), package.json
- Central package management: Directory.Packages.props with VersionOverride
- 2-step OSV lookup: querybatch for IDs, then /v1/vulns/{id} for details
- Graceful degradation: warns on network errors, doesn't fail the scan
- Reuses existing Finding type, output formatters, and CI integration
- New --ecosystem flag to filter by ecosystem (e.g., nuget,npm)

Modules:
- src/Deps/ManifestParser.fs — discovers and parses package manifests
- src/Deps/OsvClient.fs — OSV.dev HTTP client with batch queries
- src/Deps/DepScanner.fs — orchestrates parsing → querying → normalization

Tested against ZTS: 402 deps across 34 manifests, 7 real CVEs found
with severity levels and fix version suggestions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Bump version to 1.1.48

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
